It is recommended to include the accepted file types and maximum file size in the subtitle.

properties.name: multipleFiles
properties.description: Whether to allow multiple file uploads

properties.name: acceptedFileTypes
properties.description:
The accepted file types for upload, e.g. ['image/png', 'image/gif', 'image/svg+xml']

If not provided, all file types are accepted.

properties.name: maxFileSize
properties.description: The maximum file size allowed for upload in MB. If not provided, defaults to 2 MB.

properties.name: files
properties.description:
The files currently being uploaded. This is updated as files are being uploaded.

If not provided, the component will not display any files upload items.

properties.name: onUpload

properties.name: onError
properties.description: The function called when an error occurs during upload

properties.name: leading
properties.description:
The leading element to display in the ListItem.

Leading elements should only be one of the following [Icon](/icons), Img, Avatar.

properties.name: label
properties.description: The label to display in the ListItem.

properties.name: subText
properties.description: The subtext to display in the ListItem.

properties.name: trailing
properties.description:
The trailing element to display in the ListItem.

Trailing elements should only be one of the following [Icon](/icons), Checkbox, ListItemButton, Radio, Switch, Tag, Txt.

properties.name: href
properties.description:
The [href](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/a#href) of the list item.

If the href is provided, the ListItem will render as an anchor element (`<a>`).

properties.name: innerRef
properties.description: A ref to the list item div element.

properties.name: role
properties.description:
The ARIA role of the list item.

The role will be set to 'button' automatically if the ListItem has an onClick prop and is not a button, label, or anchor element.

If including other focusable elements (e.g. buttons, links) in the leading or trailing slots, the role should be set explicitly to something other than 'button'.

properties.name: onClick
properties.description: Callback function that is called when the ListItem is clicked.

properties.name: id
properties.description:
The unique ID of the list item.

If not provided, a unique ID will be generated. This is useful for accessibility and testing purposes.

properties.name: hideAriaLabel
properties.description: Whether to hide the label from screen readers. Use this when the label is redundant with other context, such as within a ListItemMenu or Label.

properties.name: aria-selected
properties.description: Indicates the current "selected" state of the list item when used in a selectable context, such as within a ListItemMenu.

properties.name: onClick
properties.description: The function to call when the button is clicked.

properties.description: The element type to render as.

properties.name: id
properties.description: The id of the element. If not provided one will be generated.

properties.description: The ARIA role of the element.

properties.name: owner
properties.description: Identifies the parent component. Helps with styling, debugging, and/or testing purposes.

properties.name: innerRef
properties.description: A ref to the inner div element.

properties.name: children
properties.description: The items to display in the menu. These should be ListItem and Divider components.

properties.name: label
properties.description:
A label for the menu for screen readers.

This is required if the role is set to "menu" or "listbox".

properties.name: as
properties.description: The element type to render as.

properties.name: destructive
properties.description: The function of the button is destructive.

properties.name: label
properties.description: The label of the call to action button.

properties.name: onClick
properties.description: The callback function for the call to action button.

properties.name: id
properties.description: The id of the element. If not provided one will be generated.

properties.name: open
properties.description: If the dialog should appear.

properties.name: container
properties.description: The container to render the portal in.

properties.name: onClose
properties.description: Function to call when the dialog is closed.

properties.name: owner
properties.description: Identifies the parent component. Helps with styling, debugging, and/or testing purposes.

properties.name: innerRef
properties.description: A ref to the dialog element.

properties.name: disableFocusTrap
properties.description:
If focus trapping should be disabled. Generally this should not be disabled as dialogs should always trap
focus.

properties.name: header
properties.description: Modal header.

properties.name: description
properties.description: Modal description. Used for the [aria-description](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Reference/Attributes/aria-description) attribute.

properties.name: cancelButton
properties.description:
Whether to show the cancel button in the footer.

Providing a string will set the label of the cancel button.

properties.name: callToAction
properties.description: The call to action button to display in the footer of the modal.

properties.name: buttonFormat
properties.description: The format of the buttons in the footer. Ignored if not mobile.

properties.description:
The ARIA role of the list item.

The role will be set to 'button' automatically if the ListItem has an onClick prop and is not a button,
label, or anchor element.

If including other focusable elements (e.g. buttons, links) in the leading or trailing slots, the role should
be set explicitly to something other than 'button'.

properties.description: The [name](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input#name) of the control.

properties.name: size
properties.description: The size of the element.

properties.name: required
properties.description: Determines if the element is [required](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Attributes/required).

properties.name: id
properties.description: The id of the element. If not provided one will be generated.

properties.name: invalid
properties.description:
Indicates that the element is in an invalid state and displays the error theme.

If set to true, an accompanying error message should be provided.

properties.name: disabled
properties.description: Determines if the element is [disabled](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Attributes/disabled).

properties.name: readOnly
properties.description: Determines if the element is [readonly](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Attributes/readonly).

properties.name: scrollLimit
properties.description:
The maximum number of ListItems to show before scrolling is enabled.

Used in conjunction with scrollListItemsStyle utility.

properties.name: options
properties.description: Array of options to display in the select
properties.example:
[
{ id: '1', label: 'Option 1' },
{ id: '2', label: 'Option 2' },
{ id: '3', label: 'Option 3' },
{ id: '4', label: 'Option 4' },
{ id: '5', label: 'Option 5' },
{ id: '6', label: 'Option 6' },
{ id: '7', label: 'Option 7' },
{ id: '8', label: 'Option 8' },
{ id: '9', label: 'Option 9' },
{ id: '10', label: 'Option 10' },
]

properties.name: value
properties.description: Selected value

properties.name: onChange
properties.description: The function to call when the selected values change.

properties.name: placeholder
properties.description: Placeholder for the select

properties.name: variant
properties.description: The variant of the skeleton that best hints the content being loaded.

properties.name: width
properties.description: The width of the skeleton. This is ignored when variant is 'profile' or 'thumbnail'.

properties.name: height
properties.description: The height of the skeleton. This is ignored when variant is 'profile' or 'thumbnail'.

properties.name: children
properties.description:
The content of the skeleton.

When the value is undefined, null or false the skeleton will appear.

If the value is provided, the skeleton will not appear and the content will be displayed instead.

properties.name: variant
properties.description: The variant of the text being loaded.

properties.name: lines
properties.description: The number of lines showing.

properties.name: children
properties.description:
The content of the skeleton.

When the value is undefined, null or false the skeleton will appear.

If the value is provided, the skeleton will not appear and the content will be displayed instead.

properties.name: disabled
properties.description: Determines if the element is [disabled](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Attributes/disabled).

properties.name: readOnly
properties.description: Determines if the element is [readonly](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Attributes/readonly).

properties.name: label
properties.description: The label of the slider.

properties.name: value
properties.description:
The numerical value of the slider.

Providing an array of two numbers will create a **range slider**.

properties.name: onChange
properties.description: Invoked with the new value when value changes.

properties.name: min
properties.description: The minimum value of the slider.

properties.name: max
properties.description: The maximum value of the slider.;

properties.name: marks
properties.description: Indicates if marks should be displayed on the slider.

properties.name: step
properties.description: The number that specifies the granularity that the value must adhere to

properties.name: formatNumber
properties.description:
Optional function to format the display of each number.

Useful for currency, percentages, etc.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the slider input, useful for form submissions.

properties.name: id
properties.description: The id of the element. If not provided one will be generated.

properties.name: text
properties.description: Text to be shown in the snackbar

properties.name: closeButton
properties.description:
Whether to show a close button on the snackbar.

Since there is no default timeout, this is true by default. You can provide a timeout to auto-dismiss the snackbar in addition to the close button.

properties.name: closeButtonLabel
properties.description: Label for the close button.

properties.name: icon
properties.description: The icon of the button.

properties.name: onClose
properties.description: Callback when the snackbar is dismissed

properties.name: timeout
properties.description:
Time in milliseconds after which the snackbar will auto dismiss.

If no timeout is provided, and closeButton is set to false the snackbar will not be dismissable.

properties.name: open
properties.description: If the snackbar is open or not.

properties.name: innerRef
properties.description: A ref to the snackbar element.

properties.name: disableFocusTrap
properties.description: If focus trapping should be disabled. Generally this should not be disabled as snackbars should always trap focus.

properties.name: brand
properties.description: The current brand variant.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The [name](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input#name) of the control.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The value of the control.

properties.name: disabled
properties.description: Determines if the element is [disabled](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Attributes/disabled).

properties.name: onChange
properties.description: The function to call when the control is checked or unchecked.

properties.name: checked
properties.description: Marks the control as checked.

properties.name: readOnly
properties.description: Only exists as a alias for `disabled` and to match other input properties.

properties.name: label
properties.description: The label of the option. Also used as the aria-label of the control.

properties.name: description
properties.description: The description of the option.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The [name](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input#name) of the control.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The value of the control.

properties.name: disabled
properties.description: Determines if the element is [disabled](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Attributes/disabled).

properties.name: aria-label
properties.description: The aria-label for the element.

properties.name: checked
properties.description: Marks the control as checked.

properties.name: onChange
properties.description: The function to call when the control is checked or unchecked.

properties.name: readOnly
properties.description: Only exists as a alias for `disabled` and to match other input properties.

properties.name: id
properties.description: The id of the tab utility, used for accessibility.

properties.name: width
properties.description:
When 'fill' the options will fill the width of the container. When 'hug', the options will be as wide as their
content.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The value of the selected tab.

properties.name: label
properties.description: The label for the tab utility, used for accessibility.

properties.name: options
properties.description:
The tabs to display.

If **less than 2** items are provided, the component will not render.

properties.name: size
properties.description: The size of the tabs.

properties.name: onChange
properties.description: The function to call when the tab is clicked.

properties.name: showTrail
properties.description: When width is 'hug' this determines if the trailing underline should be showing. When width is 'fill' this property isn't applicable.

properties.name: key
properties.description: The key of the column. This is used to access the data in the row.

properties.name: label
properties.description: The label of the column. This is used to display the column header.

properties.name: width
properties.description:
The width of the column. This is used to set the width of the column.

Used to set the value of
[grid-template-columns](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/grid-template-columns)

properties.name: align
properties.description: The horizontal alignment of the column. This is used to set the text alignment of the column.

properties.name: valign
properties.description: The vertical alignment of the column. This is used to set the text alignment of the column.

properties.name: sort
properties.description:
The sorting function for the column.

This can be a custom sorting function or one of the built-in sorting functions:

`string`, `boolean`, `date`, or `number`.

If unspecified, the column will not be sortable.

properties.name: formatter
properties.description:
A formatter function for the cell values in the column.

This function is called for each cell in the column and can be used to customize the display of the cell value.

properties.name: data
properties.description:
The data of the table.

Array<TableRow>

properties.name: columns
properties.description: The column definitions of the table.

properties.name: title
properties.description: The title of the table.

properties.name: size
properties.description: The size of the table.

properties.name: pageSize
properties.description:
The number of rows per page.

If the number of rows exceeds the page size, pagination controls will be displayed.

properties.name: options
properties.description:
The tabs to display.

If **less than 2** items are provided, the component will not render.
properties.example:
[
{ value: '1', label: 'Option 1' },
{ value: '2', label: 'Disabled 2 ', disabled: true },
{ value: '3', label: 'Option 3' },
]

properties.name: skeletonLines
properties.description:
The number of lines to display for skeleton text.

If not provided, it defaults to 0, meaning no skeleton lines will be shown.

properties.name: inherit
properties.description: Inherit style and not set font style.

properties.name: children
properties.description: The children elements that will have access to the UI context.

properties.name: ids
properties.description:
An array of string IDs representing the navigable elements. These IDs should correspond to the `id` attributes of
the elements in the DOM. Ensure the elements are not disabled.

properties.name: callback
properties.description:
An optional callback function that is invoked when an arrow key is pressed. This function receives the key name,
the keyboard event, and the next active element ID. If the function returns `true`, the default navigation
behavior is prevented; if it returns `false` or is not provided, the default behavior proceeds.

properties.name: increments
properties.description: Optional configuration to set navigation direction increments.

properties.name: defaultActiveId
properties.description:
The ID of the element that should be active by default. If not provided, the first ID in the `ids` array will be
used.

properties.name: placement
properties.description:
The preferred placement of the floating element.

This determines where the floating element will be positioned relative to the reference element.

properties.name: arrowRef
properties.description: A ref object for the arrow element.

properties.name: strategy
properties.description:
The positioning strategy ('absolute' or 'fixed').

When set to 'fixed', the floating element will be positioned relative to the viewport.

When set to 'absolute', the floating element will be positioned relative to the nearest positioned ancestor.

properties.name: offsetOptions
properties.description: The offset options for the floating element.

properties.name: refWidth
properties.description: When set to true, the width of the floating element will match the width of the reference element.

properties.name: hide
properties.description: Whether to hide the floating element.
